title: Concatenation of Array
hint: Create a new array of size 2n and place the original array twice.
intuition: Basic array traversal and construction.

title: Running Sum of 1d Array
hint: Keep adding the previous sum while traversing.
intuition: Every position stores cumulative information.

title: Find Numbers with Even Number of Digits
hint: Count digits for each number.
intuition: Convert numbers into digit counts.

title: Richest Customer Wealth
hint: Sum each row and track the maximum.
intuition: Simple nested traversal with max tracking.

title: Kids With the Greatest Number of Candies
hint: Compare each value after adding extra candies to the current maximum.
intuition: Check a condition for every element independently.

title: Shuffle the Array
hint: Split the array into two halves and interleave them.
intuition: This teaches how to build a result using positional mapping.

title: Build Array from Permutation
hint: Use nums[i] as the next index while building the result.
intuition: Values themselves can act as indexes.

title: Create Target Array in the Given Order
hint: Insert each value at the given index in the target array.
intuition: Practice building arrays through ordered insertions.

title: Plus One
hint: Traverse backward and handle carry like manual addition.
intuition: Digits in arrays follow normal place-value arithmetic.

title: Add to Array-Form of Integer
hint: Add the integer from the back of the array and propagate carry.
intuition: This is plus-one generalized to any integer.
commonMistakes: Mishandling carry after the array ends

title: Largest Number At Least Twice of Others
hint: Track the largest and second largest values in one scan.
intuition: A single pass is enough when you only need the top two values.
commonMistakes: Not updating the second maximum correctly

title: Check If N and Its Double Exist
hint: Use a set to check whether x*2 or x/2 has already appeared.
intuition: Hashing turns search into constant time lookups.

title: Find Pivot Index
hint: Compare left sum and right sum while scanning once.
intuition: A pivot exists when the sums on both sides are equal.

title: Pascal's Triangle
hint: Each row is built from the previous row by adding adjacent values.
intuition: Practice generating structured arrays row by row.

title: Pascal's Triangle II
hint: Build rows iteratively until you reach the required index.
intuition: This is the single-row version of Pascal generation.
commonMistakes: Modifying the row in the wrong direction

title: Maximum Subarray
hint: Restart the current sum when it becomes harmful.
intuition: Negative running sums reduce future answers.

title: Maximum Product Subarray
hint: Track both maximum and minimum products at each step.
intuition: Negative numbers can flip the sign and create a new maximum.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
hint: Track the minimum price seen so far.
intuition: Best profit depends on the cheapest earlier buy price.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
hint: Take every positive price difference.
intuition: Many small profits can outperform one long wait.

title: Degree of an Array
hint: Track frequency and first/last positions of each number.
intuition: Degree is determined by the most frequent element.
commonMistakes: Only counting frequency and ignoring range

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
hint: Maintain a slow pointer for unique elements and a fast pointer for scanning.
intuition: Sorted duplicates stay adjacent, so one pass is enough.

title: Remove Element
hint: Overwrite unwanted values with a write pointer.
intuition: Only the kept elements matter in the final array.

title: Move Zeroes
hint: Move non-zero elements forward while preserving their relative order.
intuition: Treat zeros as removable values but keep stability.

title: Merge Sorted Array
hint: Fill the answer from the back using two pointers.
intuition: Working from the end prevents overwriting useful values.

title: Squares of a Sorted Array
hint: Compare absolute values from both ends and place the larger square at the back.
intuition: Negatives can become large after squaring, so both ends matter.

title: Sort Colors
hint: Use three regions for 0, 1, and 2.
intuition: Partitioning can solve sorting-like tasks in one pass.

title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
hint: Start from both ends and move inward based on the sum.
intuition: Sorted order gives you a directional way to shrink the search space.
commonMistakes: Returning zero-based indices when one-based are required

title: Container With Most Water
hint: Move the pointer with the smaller height inward.
intuition: Width shrinks every move, so height improvement is the key.

title: Trapping Rain Water
hint: Keep left max and right max boundaries while scanning inward.
intuition: Water at a point is limited by the shorter surrounding boundary.

title: 3Sum
hint: Sort first, then fix one number and search the remaining pair with two pointers.
intuition: Sorting turns a triple search into a structured scan.

title: 3Sum Closest
hint: Fix one number and move the pair pointers based on the current sum.
intuition: This is 3Sum with a closest-target objective instead of exact matches.
commonMistakes: Not updating the best answer at every step

title: Next Permutation
hint: Find the first descending point from the right, swap, then reverse the suffix.
intuition: The next lexicographic arrangement is found by changing the smallest possible suffix.

title: Rotate Array
hint: Use the reversal trick to rotate in place.
intuition: Rotation can be done by reversing whole and partial segments.
commonMistakes: Not handling k greater than n

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II
hint: Allow each number at most twice using a slow pointer.
intuition: A small change to duplicate handling creates a deeper in-place variant.

title: Sort Array By Parity
hint: Move even numbers to the front and odd numbers to the back.
intuition: Partitioning by a simple predicate is a useful array skill.

title: Sort Array By Parity II
hint: Place evens at even indexes and odds at odd indexes.
intuition: This is index-aware partitioning.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to move both pointers after swap

title: Contains Duplicate
hint: Track seen values in a set.
intuition: Repeated values can be detected with constant-time lookup.

title: Contains Duplicate II
hint: Keep a window of the last k values using a set.
intuition: Duplicate detection with distance constraints is a windowing problem.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to remove elements outside the window

title: Majority Element
hint: Use Boyer-Moore voting.
intuition: Majority element survives pair cancellation.

title: Majority Element II
hint: There can be at most two majority candidates.
intuition: The vote algorithm generalizes to two possible winners.

title: Missing Number
hint: Use the expected sum formula or mark visited indexes.
intuition: The missing value is what the full sequence lacks.

title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
hint: Mark visited indexes by changing signs or swapping.
intuition: Missing values become visible after in-place marking.

title: Set Mismatch
hint: Use index marking to identify duplicate and missing values.
intuition: One extra value and one missing value create a mismatch in the index mapping.
commonMistakes: Missing the duplicate when it appears twice

title: Find the Duplicate Number
hint: Treat array values as next pointers and detect the cycle.
intuition: A duplicate creates a loop when you follow indexes as links.
commonMistakes: Using extra space when O(1) is possible

title: First Missing Positive
hint: Place each positive number at its correct index whenever possible.
intuition: After rearrangement, the first wrong index gives the answer.

title: Single Number
hint: XOR all elements together.
intuition: Equal pairs cancel out under XOR.

title: Single Number II
hint: Count bits at every position modulo 3.
intuition: Triplets vanish when counted per bit position.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to reconstruct the result bitwise

title: Single Number III
hint: Use XOR to separate the two unique numbers into different groups.
intuition: A distinguishing bit can split the array into two independent XOR problems.
commonMistakes: Not isolating the lowest set bit correctly

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
hint: Only start counting from numbers that do not have a predecessor.
intuition: Each consecutive run should be counted only once.
commonMistakes: Starting from every number and causing O(n^2) behavior

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
hint: Store counts of prefix sums and look for currentPrefix - k.
intuition: Past prefix sums tell you how many subarrays end here.

title: Continuous Subarray Sum
hint: Use prefix sum remainders and look for repeated remainders.
intuition: Two equal remainders form a subarray divisible by k.
commonMistakes: Not handling k equals zero separately

title: Subarray Sums Divisible by K
hint: Count prefix sum remainders modulo k.
intuition: Equal remainders imply a divisible subarray between them.

title: Maximum Size Subarray Sum Equals k
hint: Store the earliest index for each prefix sum.
intuition: The earliest occurrence gives the longest possible subarray.

title: Count Number of Nice Subarrays
hint: Count odd numbers using prefix sums or a sliding count map.
intuition: Convert the problem into counting prefixes with a target odd count.

title: Binary Subarrays With Sum
hint: Use prefix sums and count how many previous prefixes can form the target sum.
intuition: Binary values still work with prefix-sum counting.

title: Range Sum Query - Immutable
hint: Precompute prefix sums once and answer each query by subtraction.
intuition: Preprocessing can make repeated queries constant time.

title: Find the Middle Index in Array
hint: Compare left sum and right sum as you scan.
intuition: This is a pivot-style balance point problem.
commonMistakes: Not updating the right sum correctly

title: Kth Missing Positive Number
hint: Use how many numbers are missing before each index.
intuition: The gap count increases monotonically, so binary search fits naturally.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to convert between index and value

title: Search Insert Position
hint: Find the first position where the target can be inserted.
intuition: This is a lower-bound search problem.

title: Binary Search
hint: Compare the middle element and shrink the search range.
intuition: Binary search is the foundation of sorted-array reasoning.
commonMistakes: Infinite loop due to wrong boundaries

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
hint: At each step, determine which half is sorted.
intuition: Rotation breaks normal order, but one half stays sorted.
commonMistakes: Testing the wrong half for the target

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
hint: The minimum lies in the unsorted half.
intuition: You can discard the sorted half and keep the side with the pivot.

title: Find Peak Element
hint: Compare the middle element with its neighbor.
intuition: A slope direction tells you which side contains a peak.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
hint: Partition around a pivot until the target index is found.
intuition: Selection can be done without fully sorting the array.

title: Capacity To Ship Packages Within D Days
hint: Binary search the capacity and verify feasibility by simulating days.
intuition: When the answer space is monotonic, search the answer itself.

title: Split Array Largest Sum
hint: Binary search the minimum possible largest subarray sum.
intuition: Try a candidate answer, then check how many partitions are needed.
commonMistakes: Confusing partition count with subarray sum

title: Search a 2D Matrix
hint: Treat the matrix as one sorted 1D array.
intuition: A 2D grid can be searched as a flattened sorted list.

title: Search a 2D Matrix II
hint: Start from the top-right or bottom-left corner and eliminate one row or column at a time.
intuition: Monotonic structure lets you shrink the search space quickly.

title: Transpose Matrix
hint: Swap rows and columns by writing matrix[r][c] into matrix[c][r].
intuition: Transpose is direct coordinate remapping.

title: Reshape the Matrix
hint: Treat the matrix as a flat sequence and rebuild it in the new shape.
intuition: This is index mapping in two dimensions.
commonMistakes: Not checking if reshape is possible

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
hint: Use the first row and first column as markers.
intuition: In-place marking avoids extra space for row and column flags.
commonMistakes: Losing the original first row/column state

title: Spiral Matrix
hint: Keep four boundaries and shrink them after each direction.
intuition: Spiral traversal is boundary simulation in layers.

title: Spiral Matrix II
hint: Build the matrix layer by layer in spiral order.
intuition: This is Spiral Matrix turned into a construction problem.

title: Rotate Image
hint: Transpose the matrix and then reverse each row.
intuition: A rotation can be decomposed into simpler coordinate changes.
commonMistakes: Doing the transformation in the wrong order

title: Game of Life
hint: Encode the next state without destroying the current state too early.
intuition: Simulation problems often need temporary state encoding.
commonMistakes: Updating cells directly before all neighbors are processed

title: Maximum Average Subarray I
hint: Compute the first window sum and slide it by one step.
intuition: Fixed-size windows are a strong entry point for sliding window problems.
commonMistakes: Recomputing the whole window every time

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
hint: Expand until the condition is met, then shrink from the left as much as possible.
intuition: Variable windows grow and shrink based on validity.

title: Fruit Into Baskets
hint: Maintain a window with at most two fruit types.
intuition: This is a frequency-limited sliding window.
commonMistakes: Losing track of fruit counts while shrinking

title: Subarray Product Less Than K
hint: Expand the window and shrink it while the product is too large.
intuition: Window size is controlled by a multiplicative constraint.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that k <= 1 is a special case

title: Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff Less Than or Equal to Limit
hint: Track the current window max and min with two deques.
intuition: Deque structures make sliding max/min efficient.
commonMistakes: Using normal lists with O(n) pops

title: Corporate Flight Bookings
hint: Apply each booking range in O(1) using a difference array.
intuition: Range updates become easy when you store deltas instead of full values.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to subtract after the range ends

title: Car Pooling
hint: Mark trip start and end points, then scan the load.
intuition: Difference arrays are ideal for capacity over time or position.
commonMistakes: Mixing up start and end adjustments

title: Range Addition
hint: Use the difference array to apply updates in constant time.
intuition: A final prefix pass rebuilds the updated array.
commonMistakes: Not rebuilding the result with a prefix scan

title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
hint: Sum both diagonals and avoid counting the center twice.
intuition: Diagonal traversal is simple coordinate-based iteration.
commonMistakes: Double counting the center in odd-sized matrices

title: Toeplitz Matrix
hint: Check whether each diagonal has equal values.
intuition: Matrix pattern validation trains careful 2D traversal.
